metrics.definition: Host public cost, repeatable venue economics, and risk of post-Games debt.
metrics.direction: higher is lower burden and better long-run value

metrics.definition: Need for new land, temporary construction, and reuse of existing venues.
metrics.direction: higher is less new land pressure

metrics.definition: Athlete, resident, volunteer, and spectator experience.
Travel burden, emissions exposure, and operational complexity.
Learning curve, infrastructure legacy, and capacity to improve future Games.
Prestige, local identity, and incentive for cities or nations to participate.
Ability to keep the Olympics globally meaningful and inclusive.

ranked_strategies.description: Rotate among vetted regional host networks that reuse existing venues and share operations.
Keep rotating host selection but require compact venues, cost caps, and public legacy guarantees.
Award event clusters to multiple cities with existing venues under one Games brand.
Split sports into Winter, Spring, Summer, and Fall Games to reduce the load on any one host.
Select one recurring Summer host and one recurring Winter host with permanent Olympic infrastructure.

recommended_strategy.description: Rotate among vetted regional host networks that reuse existing venues and share operations.

strategy_evaluation.selection_rule: Maximize weighted impact, feasibility, and bid attractiveness while penalizing governance complexity and long transition time.

milestones.action: Adopt the seven-dimension Olympic impact scorecard and publish scoring rules before accepting bids.
Identify candidate host networks with existing venues, transit capacity, and public legacy commitments.
Run selected youth, qualifier, or test events under the recommended governance model.
Write cost caps, reuse rules, and public reporting into host contracts.
Choose the first Games cycle using regional_rotation_with_reuse if scorecard performance remains above the status quo benchmark.
Publish the first three-cycle rotation plan and annual legacy audit schedule.

one_page_ioc_memo:
To the IOC and COMAP Interdisciplinary Committee on Modern Games:

The core risk in the official 2023 ICM-F statement is that fewer cities are willing to bid because the Games can impose high short- and long-term burdens. I recommend adopting a regional rotation with venue reuse as the first policy path. It keeps host prestige and global unity while reducing new land pressure and public cost. The scorecard evaluates each option on economic burden, land use, human satisfaction, travel, future improvement, host prestige, and unity through sport. The recommended strategy scores 0.534 overall and can be moved through 6 governance milestones before the first full cycle decision. Permanent Summer/Winter sites and four seasonal Games remain useful contingency designs, but they require heavier governance changes and may weaken local prestige or calendar coherence. The immediate IOC action is to publish the metric charter, shortlist reusable host networks, and make future host contracts auditable on cost caps, public legacy, and venue reuse.

assumption_audit.truthfulness_note: This workflow uses official statement parameters from the COMAP PDF and explicit deterministic planning assumptions; it does not use random placeholder data.

assumptions.score_scale: All strategy and metric values are deterministic 0-1 planning scores derived from official statement criteria.
assumptions.no_attached_data: The official problem supplies no CSV/XLSX attachment; the PDF asks teams to build metrics and compare policy strategies.
assumptions.timeline: Timeline years measure IOC governance and host-contract transition time, not construction duration for a specific city.
